nodes.name: fractional_differentiator
nodes.description: Computes the fractional differentiation of a time series. It first calculates the necessary weights based on the differentiation order 'd' and then applies them to the series, dropping terms below a specified threshold.

inputs.name: d
inputs.constraints: The order of differentiation, where 0 <= d <= 1.

inputs.name: threshold
inputs.constraints: Threshold for dropping small weights to control memory usage. A smaller value means more terms are kept.

nodes.type_signature: (series: pd.Series, d: float, threshold: float) -> pd.Series
nodes.conceptual_summary: long_range_dependency_attenuator. Applies a convolution with a power-law kernel to a 1D ordered sequence. This transform acts as a tunable high-pass filter, systematically attenuating low-frequency components (i.e., long-term trends or memory) based on a continuous input parameter. The operation effectively generalizes integer-order differencing to a fractional degree, allowing fine-grained control over the memory preserved in the output sequence.. Applications: Filtering low-frequency drift from geophysical sensor data (e.g., seismic or GPS time series) to isolate high-frequency event signals., Normalizing gene expression data along a chromosome to remove large-scale regional biases, thereby highlighting local areas of significant regulation., In fluid dynamics, analyzing time-series data from a velocity probe to separate large-scale turbulent structures from small-scale fluctuations., Preprocessing sequential feature vectors for a machine learning model to induce stationarity, which can improve model stability and predictive performance.
